USER: Reviewer feedback (round 4). Now the architecture must be right:
1. Dependency injection everywhere: core logic must NOT call the filesystem or clock directly. Inject a Store interface (load/save), a Clock, and an output io.Writer; wire real impls only in main().
2. Pure core / thin shell: command logic operates on an in-memory task-list value; I/O at the edges.
3. Tests: table-driven, using an in-memory Store + a fake Clock (no real files / wall-clock in unit tests).
4. Use sentinel errors (ErrNotFound) wrapped with %w. No global mutable state; construct the app from its deps. Stdlib-only (no third-party deps); hand-rolled subcommand dispatch.
[tool] Skill(args="Round 4 reviewer feedback: dependency injection everywhere (Store interface, Clock, io.Writer), pure core/thin she[truncated]) â†’ exit 0 | Launching skill: superpowers:receiving-code-review
ASSISTANT: Reading the feedback carefully before touching code.

**Item 1 (DI):** Current `Store` struct calls `os.ReadFile`/`atomicWrite` directly inside business logic methods like `Add` and `Complete`. `time.Now()` is called directly in `Add` and `Complete`. Output goes straight to `os.Stdout` inside display functions. All three are real coupling problems â€” the DI ask is correct.

**Item 2 (Pure core):** `App` should own the in-memory slice; `TaskStore` interface is `Load/Save` only. Business logic methods mutate in-memory, then call `Save` â€” no filesystem calls inside loops/lookups. That's the right boundary.

**Item 3 (Table-driven + MemStore/FakeClock):** Current tests write real files and call `time.Now()`. `TestComplete` can't assert the exact `CompletedAt` value. These are real test quality problems.

**Item 4 (Sentinel errors, no global state, hand-rolled dispatch):** `fmt.Errorf("todo %d not found", id)` isn't sentinelized â€” callers can't distinguish error types. The `var RealClock` in the previous round was a mutable global. Dispatch is already a switch/case, so "hand-rolled" is already satisfied â€” just needs to become a map of `cmdFunc` for cleanliness.

All four items are technically sound. Implementing now.
[tool] Skill(args="Go dependency injection interfaces Store Clock io.Writer table-driven tests sentinel errors", skill="recall") â†’ exit 0 | Launching skill: recall
ASSISTANT: **Ask:** Full architectural refactor â€” `TaskStore` interface, `Clock` interface, `io.Writer` injection, pure core `App` struct, sentinel errors, table-driven tests with `MemStore`/`FakeClock`, no global state, dispatch map.

**Situation:** `/private/tmp/epitest/cold-ws` â€” one large `todo.go` (Store struct with filesystem calls in business methods, `time.Now()` in `Add`/`Complete`, `os.Stdout` in display), `todo_test.go` (40 tests using real temp dirs). 40 tests currently pass.

**Plan:**
1. Create `todo.go` (types + sentinel errors), `store.go` (interfaces + FileStore + Clock), `core.go` (App + pure business logic), `display.go` (printTable with injected `io.Writer` + `today time.Time`), `main.go` (DataDir + dispatch map + command handlers)
2. `TaskStore` interface: `Load/Save/Exists`; `Clock` interface: `Now()`; no global vars
3. `App.clock.Now()` replaces all `time.Now()` calls in business logic
4. Sentinel errors `ErrNotFound`, `ErrAlreadyDone`, `ErrNothingToUndo` with `%w` wrapping
5. Rewrite `todo_test.go`: `MemStore`, `FakeClock`, `newTestApp`, full table-driven coverage

[tool] Bash(command="go build -o todo . && \\\n  NO_COLOR=1 ./todo add -p high \"write tests\" && \\\n  NO_COLOR=1 ./todo add -p low[truncated]) â†’ exit 0 | Added #1: write tests
ASSISTANT: 43/43, clean build, end-to-end working. Here's what changed architecturally:

**Five files instead of one:**
- `todo.go` â€” domain types (`Task`, `Priority`, `Status`, `StatsResult`) and sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrAlreadyDone`, `ErrNothingToUndo`)
- `store.go` â€” `TaskStore` interface (`Load/Save/Exists`), `FileStore` (atomic writes), `Clock` interface, `RealClock` struct (no global var â€” zero state)
- `core.go` â€” `App` struct with injected `store/undo/archive TaskStore`, `clock Clock`, `out io.Writer`, `useColor bool`; all business logic; no `os.*` or `time.Now()` calls
- `display.go` â€” `printTable(w io.Writer, tasks []Task, useColor bool, today time.Time)` â€” `today` injected so it's deterministic in tests
- `main.go` â€” `DataDir()`, `commands map[string]cmdFunc`, `dispatch()`, wire-up in `main()`, all `cmd*` handlers

**DI guarantees:** `App.clock.Now()` is the only clock call in business logic. Filesystem only touches `FileStore`. Output only writes to `a.out`. `NewApp` receives all three stores + clock + writer as constructor params.

**Sentinel errors:** every not-found path returns `fmt.Errorf("task %d: %w", id, ErrNotFound)` â€” callers use `errors.Is`.

**Tests:** `MemStore` (no files), `FakeClock` (fixed `epoch`), `newTestApp` helper. `TestComplete` asserts `CompletedAt == epoch` exactly â€” only possible with an injected clock. Table-driven for Add, ErrNotFound, Start, Sort, Search, IsOverdue, Tag, Stats.
